run.note: MEASURE (post-run, report-only): checks.post only asserts the mp4 + contact PNG are non-empty, never that the mp4 is a well-formed container with both tracks above a sane duration floor AND actually normalized, NOR that the contact PNG actually corresponds to THIS run's mp4 (make-contact-sheet.mjs's call site swallows its own errors, so a stale prior-run PNG can ship untouched). ffprobes the DELIVERABLE + independently re-measures its loudness (ffmpeg loudnorm, same I=-16/TP=-1 target render-complete-lesson.mjs itself aims for) + cross-checks the contact PNG's mtime against the mp4's own AND the sidecar `-contact.json` legend's totalDuration/fps against the mp4's measured duration + confirms render-timing.json recorded a completed loudnorm re-measure step (folded in from the former standalone `render-loudnorm-completed` NATIVE gate, whose literal arg-scoped lessonId path tokens made `resolveDeep` drop the whole op — DARK on every real run, since run args persist only on post-arg-persistence runs; folding it in here lets it derive lessonId in-script the same way the mp4/contact paths already do), and writes a pass/fail-per-check report — fires on a corrupt, silent, truncated, un-normalized, un-loudnorm-verified, or stale/mismatched-contact-sheet render that checks.post alone would miss.

measure.note: All 9 measure-render.mjs checks (exists/ffprobe-ran/has-video/has-audio/duration-floor/lufs-within-tolerance/contact-freshness/contact-duration-match/loudnorm-completed) must pass; `failedChecks` in the report names which one didn't. contactFreshness+contactDurationMatch close the stale-contact-sheet hole: a PNG left over from a failed/skipped regen either has an older mtime than the freshly-rendered mp4, or its `-contact.json` legend's implied duration no longer matches the mp4's actual duration. loudnormCompleted closes the un-normalized-master hole: render-complete-lesson.mjs's loudnorm block swallows its own failure, so the 'Loudnorm verify (re-measure)' step's presence in render-timing.json is the only artifact-level signal the deliverable actually landed the -16 LUFS/-1 dBTP target.
